attributes.mlflow.spanOutputs: {"trajectory": {"thought_0": "I need to find out which major river is closest to Buford, North Dakota. This information might be available on Wikipedia or other geographical resources. I will search for the major rivers in North Dakota and their proximity to Buford.", "tool_name_0": "search_wikipedia", "tool_args_0": {"query": "major rivers near Buford, North Dakota"}, "observation_0": ["Fort Buford | Fort Buford was a United States Army Post at the confluence of the Missouri and Yellowstone Rivers in Dakota Territory, present day North Dakota, and the site of Sitting Bull's surrender in 1881.", "Buford, North Dakota | Buford is an unincorporated community in Williams County, North Dakota, United States. It is the nearest community to the Fort Union Trading Post National Historic Site. It is named for the nearby historic Fort Buford at the confluence of the Missouri and Yellowstone Rivers.", "Playford River (Northern Territory) | Playford River is a river in the Northern Territory of Australia and has a length of 406 km. The river originates near the base of Mount Morgan then flows in a southerly direction before veering west and eventually dicharging into Lake De Burgh. The Playford has three tributaries: Buchanan Creek, Desert Creek and Eastern Creek—as well as a number of permanent waterholes. These include Tobacco waterhole, Ibis waterhole, Upper and Lower Amazon Lagoon and Xmas hole."], "thought_1": "The search results indicate that Buford, North Dakota is near the confluence of the Missouri and Yellowstone Rivers. This suggests that the Missouri River is the major river closest to Buford. I will finish the task now as I have the necessary information.", "tool_name_1": "finish", "tool_args_1": {}, "observation_1": "Completed."}, "reasoning": "Buford, North Dakota is located near the confluence of the Missouri and Yellowstone Rivers. The information gathered indicates that the Missouri River is the major river closest to Buford, making it the most relevant answer to the question.", "answer": "The major river nearest to Buford, North Dakota is the Missouri River."}

{"signature": "StringSignature(question, trajectory -> next_thought, next_tool_name, next_tool_args
    instructions="Given the fields `question`, produce the fields `answer`.\n\nYou are an Agent. In each episode, you will be given the fields `question` as input.     next_tool_args = Field(annotation=dict[str, Any] required=True json_schema_extra={'__dspy_field_type': 'output', 'prefix': 'Next Tool Args:', 'desc': '${next_tool_args}'})

attributes.mlflow.spanOutputs: ["Fort Buford | Fort Buford was a United States Army Post at the confluence of the Missouri and Yellowstone Rivers in Dakota Territory, present day North Dakota, and the site of Sitting Bull's surrender in 1881.", "Buford, North Dakota | Buford is an unincorporated community in Williams County, North Dakota, United States. It is the nearest community to the Fort Union Trading Post National Historic Site. It is named for the nearby historic Fort Buford at the confluence of the Missouri and Yellowstone Rivers.", "Playford River (Northern Territory) | Playford River is a river in the Northern Territory of Australia and has a length of 406 km. The river originates near the base of Mount Morgan then flows in a southerly direction before veering west and eventually dicharging into Lake De Burgh. The Playford has three tributaries: Buchanan Creek, Desert Creek and Eastern Creek—as well as a number of permanent waterholes. These include Tobacco waterhole, Ibis waterhole, Upper and Lower Amazon Lagoon and Xmas hole."]
